report_name: TanStack Details Sophisticated Supply Chain Attack That Compromised 42 npm Packages

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Attackers submitted a malicious pull request to a GitHub Actions workflow that used the insecure 'pull_request_target' trigger. The executed code then poisoned the shared GitHub Actions cache with a malicious payload.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The malicious payload, executed from the poisoned cache within the CI/CD environment, stole a short-lived OIDC token used for passwordless publishing to npm.

attack_patterns.name: Subvert Trust Controls
attack_patterns.description: The stolen OIDC token was used to authenticate to npm and publish malicious versions of 42 TanStack packages, bypassing normal trust and authentication mechanisms.

malware.name: Unnamed Credential Harvester
malware.description: A credential-stealing payload delivered via a GitHub Actions cache poisoning attack. It was designed to steal cloud credentials (AWS, GCP), Kubernetes tokens, GitHub tokens, SSH keys, and npm configurations from developer environments and CI/CD runners. It also included a self-propagation mechanism.

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure GitHub Actions pull_request_target Trigger
vulnerabilities.description: An insecure configuration in a GitHub Actions workflow using the 'pull_request_target' trigger allowed code from a malicious pull request from a fork to execute with elevated permissions, enabling the cache poisoning stage of the attack.

identities.name: TanStack
identities.description: The open-source project whose npm packages were compromised in the supply chain attack.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The public package registry where 84 malicious package versions were published using a stolen OIDC token.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The platform hosting the source code and the CI/CD service (GitHub Actions) that was exploited in the attack.

infrastructure.name: Poisoned GitHub Actions Cache
infrastructure.description: The shared GitHub Actions cache was used as a staging area. Attackers wrote a malicious payload to the cache, which was then executed by a subsequent, legitimate build workflow.

indicators.name: Malicious Access to .npmrc Configuration File
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious Access to .npmrc Configuration File
id: 2b9c1a8e-1f3a-4d5b-8c6d-7e9f0a1b2c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to the .npmrc file, which may indicate theft of npm authentication tokens as seen in the TanStack supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://tech.wrapt.dev/posts/tanstack-details-sophisticated-npm-supply-chain-attack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-19
tags:
  - attack.t1555
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\.npmrc'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to the .npmrc file, which was a target of the credential harvesting malware in the TanStack supply chain attack.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
